title: Five-Axis Article Classification
description: Epistemic metadata for an article in an integral knowledge corpus. Implements Pattern III of the Integral Knowledge Architecture methodology. The five axes are orthogonal — each combination tells you something the others cannot.

doctrinal_status.description: How clearly does the system see what this article claims? Ajna register, light vocabulary. 'clouded' — seeing still forming. 'clear' — settled, default tier. 'luminous' — illuminates adjacent territory; rare, earned, never declared.

content_layer.description: Editorial register. 'canon' — intemporal metaphysical architecture; no external citations. 'bridge' — connects doctrine to modern science, specific traditions, contemporary findings. 'applied' — commentary, protocols, analysis engaging the world. 'paper' — academic-paper register, citation as interlocutor.

breadth.description: What proportion of the article's intended territory has been claimed. 'partial' — skeleton or significant territory uncovered. 'substantial' — most territory covered with identifiable gaps. 'full' — all intended sections present.

depth.description: How thoroughly the article penetrates the territory it has claimed. 'introductory' — covers essentials, coherent first orientation. 'developed' — real engagement with complexity. 'comprehensive' — approaches the fullness of what the system intends to say.

craft.description: Quality of making. Vishuddha register, water vocabulary. 'muddy' — channel not cut cleanly; fails at least one of precision/compression/coherence/register/sovereignty/transmissibility. 'clean' — passes every dimension at minimum threshold. 'pure' — reference quality; rare, earned, never declared.
